task_completion.description: Load both DICOM series into Weasis and display them side-by-side
task_completion.visual_evidence: Weasis is open and displaying two image series simultaneously in a side-by-side layout.

task_completion.description: Apply different window/level settings to simulate bone and soft tissue windows
task_completion.visual_evidence: The two displayed images in Weasis show noticeably different contrast/brightness levels, indicating an attempt to apply bone and soft tissue windowing.

task_completion.description: Perform line measurements on both displayed images
task_completion.visual_evidence: Line measurement annotations with length values (in mm) are clearly visible on both of the images displayed in Weasis.

task_completion.description: Export the side-by-side comparison view to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Weasis export dialog is visible with the destination path set to /home/ga/DICOM/exports/comparison_view.png, or a file manager/image viewer shows the successfully exported image at that path.

task_completion.description: Record measurements and clinical correlation in a text report
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open displaying the file /home/ga/DICOM/exports/comparison_report.txt, which contains measurement values in mm and text representing a clinical correlation.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Weasis GUI to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Weasis application open with the DICOM images loaded and manipulated, rather than relying solely on terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: The measurements in the report match the GUI annotations
integrity.visual_evidence: The numerical values (in mm) typed into the text report match the values shown on the line measurement annotations in Weasis.

integrity.description: The exported image is a genuine output from the viewer
integrity.visual_evidence: The exported comparison_view.png appears to be a real screenshot or export from Weasis showing the side-by-side DICOM images, not a generic or fabricated image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task asks the agent to compare CT and MRI scans, but the actual DICOM files provided in the directories are X-Ray Angiography (XA) series. The agent should still load these files into Weasis, display them side-by-side, apply different windowing to simulate bone/soft tissue, and perform line measurements. Do not penalize the agent if the images look like X-Rays instead of CT/MRI, as long as they fulfill the layout, windowing, measurement, and export requirements.
